refnamediv.refpurpose: Compute distance to the nearest background pixel.

listitem.para: Two-dimensional Boolean or numeric binary image. Nonzero pixels are foreground and zero-valued pixels are background.
Distance metric: "euclidean" (default), "cityblock", or "chessboard". Numeric values 2, 1, and 3 respectively are also accepted.
Double matrix with the same size as image. Background pixels are zero and foreground values increase with distance from the nearest background pixel. Values are normalized to the range [0, 1].

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Computes an OpenCV distance transform of a binary image. Distance peaks occur near the centers of foreground objects, making the result useful for constructing reliable foreground markers before watershed segmentation.

refsection.title: Examples
Reference
refsection.para: Example workflow adapted from the official OpenCV distance-transform and watershed tutorial.
